steps.title: Identify the evidence shift
steps.content:
Text 2 confirms the Polynesian origin claim yet signals a critical evolution in the evidence.

Storey's unique genetic mutation—the original basis—was weakened (shown to occur worldwide), but Luzuriaga-Neira's discovery of different genetic markers shared only by South American and Polynesian breeds now provides stronger support for the same conclusion.

steps.title: Match to the answer's logic
steps.content:
D captures this response: Luzuriaga-Neira's evidence is stronger and more decisive than Storey's original mutation evidence.

The others either misread what the claim says (B suggests it denies reverse transfer; C assumes European genetic uniformity) or falsely attribute disagreement Text 2 doesn't express (A claims it disputes the timeline).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
